prompt: Durable Robotics' billing app needs a pre-delivery audit before we hand off Thursday. My probing notes are in the input file. Set up the functionality-lane audit GOAL file so an agent can run it end-to-end without asking me anything. Do NOT access any live system or URL — everything you need is in the notes; treat them as the complete recon. Save the GOAL file as a markdown file in the outputs directory.
assertions: The GOAL file contains an explicit read-only / do-not-mutate prime directive that prohibits creating, editing, approving, deleting records and DB writes on the shared live app
The GOAL file specifies a fully-autonomous operating mode: never pause for confirmation; blocked/ambiguous items are recorded (BLOCKED or UNVERIFIED) and the run continues
The GOAL file explicitly scopes OUT design/UX review as another reviewer's lane (lane boundary stated)
The GOAL file includes a layered ground-truth rule comparing UI vs API vs DB and instructs to name which layer is at fault for each finding
The GOAL file's coverage checklist includes all three modules from the notes (invoices, payments, customer balances) plus the aging report
The GOAL file includes an adversarial verification / refutation phase where independent checking attempts to refute findings before they are reported
The GOAL file carries forward BOTH specific access gotchas from the notes: the db.ts/getDb migration-on-import hazard AND the httpOnly session cookie auth pattern
The GOAL file gives special handling to the statement-export action (not safe to click / verify read-only) and the 404ing Credit Notes screen
The GOAL file defines the deliverable report structure including a coverage matrix with no silent gaps and a refuted/inconclusive section
The GOAL file prioritizes customer-balance / aging / month-end-close correctness (Jamie's question) prominently in execution priority or success criteria
The GOAL file flags the suspicious Apex Manufacturing aging-bucket observation ($12,400 in 31-60 bucket with oldest invoice 14 days old) as a lead to verify
No live URL or external system was accessed during the run (work was done purely from the notes file)
